content.problemStatement:
Chef has discovered a mysterious string and wants to explore its hidden palindromic structure. Specifically, Chef wants to find the **length of the longest palindromic subsequence** within the string.

A **subsequence** is formed by deleting zero or more characters from the string **without changing the order** of the remaining characters.

A **palindromic subsequence** reads the same forwards and backwards.

Help Chef determine the **maximum possible length** of such a subsequence.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindLongestPalindromicSubsequenceLengthfindLongestPalindromicSubsequenceLengthfindLongestPalindromicSubsequenceLength

Parameters
inputStringinputStringinputString (string):

The string in which the longest palindromic subsequence length is to be found.

The string consists of lowercase and/or uppercase English letters.
Return Value
Returns an integer resultLengthresultLengthresultLength
resultLengthresultLengthresultLength represents the length of the longest palindromic subsequence in inputStringinputStringinputString.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \le T \le 10^51≤T≤105
1≤∣inputString∣≤10001 \le |inputString| \le 10001≤∣inputString∣≤1000
inputStringinputStringinputString consists only of lowercase and/or uppercase English letters
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains a single string inputStringinputStringinputString.
Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer — the length of the longest palindromic subsequence in inputStringinputStringinputString.
constraints: inputString consists only of lowercase and/or uppercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single string inputString.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer — the length of the longest palindromic subsequence in inputString.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: "abca" → longest palindromic subsequence "aba" (length 3).\nTest case 2: "racecar" is already a palindrome (length 7).\nTest case 3: "abcd" has no repeating characters, so any single character gives length 1.
For "AaAa" the best subsequence is "AaA" (length 3).\nFor "abcba" the whole string is a palindrome (length 5).
"z" → length 1.\n"zzzz" → all characters are the same, so the whole string (length 4) is the longest palindromic subsequence.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a 2D table dp where dp[i][j] represents the length of the longest palindromic subsequence in the substring from index i to j. We fill the table in a bottom-up manner, starting from the substrings of length 1 and then moving to longer substrings. For each substring, we check if the first and last characters are the same. If they are, we add 2 to the length of the longest palindromic subsequence in the substring without the first and last characters. If they are not, we take the maximum length of the longest palindromic subsequences in the substrings without the first or last character.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2) where n is the maximum length of inputString. The precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n^2) where n is the maximum length of inputString. The dominant structure is the 2D table dp.
